name: Florida Dept of Corrections (FDC)
tagline: The nation's third-largest state prison system — ~80K+ inmates across 140+ facilities and ~24K staff — running a multi-year offender-management and IT modernization push with large, recurring needs in facility-security tech, networking, and infrastructure.
overview: The Florida Department of Corrections (FDC) is the largest state agency by personnel and operates the third-largest state prison system in the United States, behind only Texas and the federal Bureau of Prisons. The department incarcerates roughly 80,000–90,000+ inmates across 140+ facilities — major institutions, annexes, work camps, work-release centers, and forestry/road camps — and supervises well over 100,000 offenders in the community through probation and parole. FDC employs about 24,000 people, the majority of them certified correctional and probation officers. For a technology seller, FDC reads as a high-volume infrastructure and security-modernization account rather than a procurement conduit: it runs its own data systems, facility-security technology, networking, and offender-management platforms, and is in the middle of a multi-year modernization of legacy systems. Leadership is in transition — long-serving Secretary Ricky Dixon announced his retirement effective June 30, 2026 — so the relationship map at the top of the agency is being rebuilt heading into FY2026-27.

budget.intro: FDC is a large direct spender. Its FY2026-27 request carried significant technology and facility-security line items on top of a major correctional-officer pay package, and the Governor's recommendations earmarked tens of millions specifically for IT modernization. Note the state's overall FY2026-27 budget (~$114.5B) had been sent to Governor DeSantis but not yet signed as of late June 2026 — confirm enacted figures once the budget is law and any vetoes are issued.

stats.label: Data-systems modernization
stats.note: FY2026-27 request for ongoing modernization of FDC's data systems

stats.label: Security equipment
stats.note: FY2026-27 request — body scanners, body-worn cameras, and related security tech

stats.label: Staff pay raise
stats.note: FY2026-27 request to lift base pay toward $28/hr across uniformed and non-uniformed staff

notes: Governor's FY2026 recommendations included ~$17M for the offender-based IT (OBIS) modernization project and ~$14M for an IT resolution plan focused on applications.
Additional FY2026-27 asks included $3.5M to expand intelligence/contraband-detection capabilities and $3.3M to replace radios, radio towers, and associated systems.
Budget figures here are requests and Governor's recommendations — the enacted FY2026-27 appropriation governs actual spend; re-verify after signing/vetoes.

initiatives.name: OBIS modernization & CIMS rollout
initiatives.summary: FDC is modernizing its legacy Offender-Based Information System (OBIS), including the launch of the Corrections Inmate Management System (CIMS) and foundational data infrastructure and governance for statewide analytics. A multi-year platform shift that pulls through compute, storage, data-platform, and integration needs.

initiatives.name: Facility security technology
initiatives.summary: Expanded body-worn camera deployment, inmate body scanners, biometric scanning (in partnership with FDLE), and contraband interdiction — including public support for FCC cellphone-jamming authority. Edge, networking, and storage at 140+ sites underpin these programs.

initiatives.name: Records & infrastructure modernization
initiatives.summary: Enterprise records modernization (OnBase) is digitizing inmate and personnel records for security, accessibility, and compliance — part of a broader move off legacy systems toward modern, mobile-enabled platforms. Networking and IT-modernization scope across a large, distributed facility footprint.

initiatives.name: Intelligence & contraband detection
initiatives.summary: FY2026-27 request to expand intelligence capabilities using modern technologies to identify and remove contraband, plus radio/tower refresh. Data, analytics, and ruggedized edge/comms infrastructure across institutions.

procurement.intro: FDC buys through Florida's centralized purchasing framework plus its own agency solicitations. The Department of Management Services (DMS) is the only entity authorized to establish State Term Contracts, and all vendors register and transact through MyFloridaMarketPlace (MFMP). Confirm Dell's State Term Contract / IT enterprise-agreement coverage first, then track FDC's direct RFPs (e.g., the offender-based information system modernization solicitations).

vehicles.name: DMS State Term Contracts
vehicles.detail: DMS is the sole authority for Florida State Term Contracts and IT enterprise agreements; agencies including FDC buy off these pre-competed vehicles. Confirm Dell coverage and scope.

vehicles.name: MyFloridaMarketPlace (MFMP)
vehicles.detail: Florida's eProcurement and PO/invoicing system run by DMS. Mandatory vendor registration and the channel for solicitation responses via the SAP Business Network.

vehicles.name: FDC agency RFPs
vehicles.detail: FDC issues its own solicitations for larger or specialized scopes — e.g., the Offender-Based Information System modernization RFP — outside or alongside State Term Contracts.

vehicles.name: Cooperative purchasing
vehicles.detail: Florida public entities also leverage cooperative/national vehicles; verify which co-ops FDC will accept for a given technology category before forecasting.

powerStructure.name: Secretary (in transition)
powerStructure.title: Agency head, FDC
powerStructure.note: Long-serving Secretary Ricky Dixon announced retirement effective June 30, 2026; a successor has been named by the Governor's office. Confirm the current Secretary and rebuild the top relationship before forecasting.

powerStructure.name: Kenneth Kicia
powerStructure.title: Chief Information Officer (FDC)
powerStructure.note: Appointed FDC CIO in early 2025 (prior Leidos VP of IT operations; former U.S. Navy officer). Owns the IT-modernization agenda — the core technology relationship.

powerStructure.name: Office of the CIO / IT
powerStructure.title: Enterprise IT & application modernization
powerStructure.note: Owns OBIS/CIMS modernization, records digitization, and the application-resolution plan — the buying center for compute, storage, and data platforms.

powerStructure.name: Security / Institutions leadership
powerStructure.title: Facility security & operations
powerStructure.note: Owns body-camera, scanner, biometric, and contraband programs across 140+ sites — the buying center for edge and networking at the institution level.

powerStructure.name: DMS State Purchasing
powerStructure.title: Statewide procurement authority
powerStructure.note: Establishes State Term Contracts and runs MFMP — the contractual path onto which FDC purchases are mapped.

news.title: Secretary Ricky Dixon retires effective June 30 after ~30 years; Governor names successor
news.impact: Top-of-agency leadership change — rebuild the executive relationship map and confirm the new Secretary's modernization priorities.

news.title: Florida Legislature sends ~$114.5B FY2026-27 budget to Governor DeSantis
news.impact: Enacted FDC IT-modernization and security-equipment dollars hinge on signing and vetoes — confirm final figures before committing forecast.

news.title: FY2026-27 request: ~$48M data-systems modernization, $18.8M security equipment
news.impact: Sizable, technology-specific asks signal continued infrastructure and facility-security spend — align Dell ISG and edge offers.

news.title: FDC publicly backs FCC proposal to jam contraband cellphones in prisons
news.impact: Signals an active security-technology agenda at the institution level — networking and edge implications across facilities.

news.title: FDC appoints Kenneth Kicia as Chief Information Officer
news.impact: New IT leadership owning OBIS/CIMS modernization — the key technology relationship to build.

dellActions: Confirm Dell's Florida DMS State Term Contract and IT enterprise-agreement coverage, and register/verify status in MyFloridaMarketPlace before pursuing FDC scopes.
Build the new relationship map post-Dixon: engage the incoming Secretary's office and CIO Kenneth Kicia around the OBIS/CIMS modernization roadmap.
Lead with ISG infrastructure — compute, storage, and data-platform — tied to the multi-year OBIS modernization and the ~$48M data-systems line.
Position edge and networking for facility-security programs (body cameras, scanners, biometrics, radio refresh) across the 140+ site footprint.
Track the enacted FY2026-27 budget (signing and vetoes) to confirm which IT-modernization and security-equipment dollars survive before forecasting.
